raw_consumer_statement:
Several months ago, I contacted Barclay to let them know I had been paying a reoccurring charge for XXXX ( I thought my son ordered and he recently moved out ). I told them I had trouble getting a number for XXXX to cancel and could they help. The Barclay representative said she did not have a number either, but to just cancel my credit card and reissue another - this would insure they could not charge. I later learned this information was incorrect and the charges kept occurring on new card. 

I finally found a way to reach XXXX I gave them last 4 numbers of credit card. I gave them expiration, etc. They assured they were not charging this account and suggested I contact the credit card company directly. 

I called Barclay back. The representative said she could only remove two months charges. She referred me to a " manager '' who would call back in one or two days. The manager called back and was less helpful. He stated he would not even give me the two months back, and if I wanted a resolution I needed to draft a letter and mail it directly to him. 

This can not be legal. I am disputing charges I did not make - and he wants a letter just to see if he will dispute them? I receive incorrect information from their representative, but nothing is done about it. I attempted several times to resolve this - calling the credit card company to alert them. They continued to charge my card monthly even though I assured them I wanted this stopped. 

I have charged tens of thousands with them. Seems odd they treat me so poorly after I pay my credit card in full and on time every month- and I am asking for probably a little over {$100.00} back.
